pulse: The industrials sector is currently trading at a median EV/EBITDA of 17.2x and a P/E of 28.7x, which positions it slightly above broader market averages. Notably, CNH Industrial (CNHI) is under pressure, reporting a profit margin of -4.4% and facing scrutiny over cost-cutting measures amid bleeding margins, aligning with the recent headlines questioning if they can stem losses and maintain competitiveness. As earnings have shown uneven results across the sector and BofA anticipates a new global industrial cycle, investors should be strategic in selecting stocks, focusing on strong fundamentals to capitalize on the expected earnings upside of 25% or more.
